steps.title: Spot the defining claim about ohmic heating
steps.content: The passage explicitly states that ohmic heating is “generally considered to be an improvement over more conventional thermal preservation methods,” then explains the specific advantage: ohmic heating generates heat throughout the food, whereas conventional methods transfer heat only from the surface inward.

steps.title: Match the claim to the choices
steps.content:
Choice B correctly identifies what the passage does: it introduces ohmic heating as a preservation technology and points to a specific advantage—the way it generates heat throughout the food, unlike conventional methods.

The other three misread the text:
  • A invents unmentioned flaws
  • C frames the content as a research overview
  • D claims the passage explains conventional methods' popularity
, which it does not.

That matches choice B — B is correct.
